meta.description: Guidelines for building C# applications Triggers on: **/*.cs

original_definition.skill_description: Guidelines for building C# applications Triggers on: **/*.cs

actions.description: Write clear and concise comments for each function.

actions.description: Demonstrate how to organize code using feature folders or domain-driven design principles.

actions.description: Guide users through implementing authentication using JWT Bearer tokens.

rules.description: Always use the latest version C#, currently C# 14 features.
Ensure that the final return statement of a method is on its own line.
Ensure that XML doc comments are created for any public APIs. When applicable, include `<example>` and `<code>` documentation in the comments.
Declare variables non-nullable, and check for `null` at entry points.
Always use `is null` or `is not null` instead of `== null` or `!= null`.
Trust the C# null annotations and don't add null checks when the type system says a value cannot be null.
Always include test cases for critical paths of the application.
Show how to implement health checks and readiness probes.

directives.description: Make only high confidence suggestions when reviewing code changes.
For libraries or external dependencies, mention their usage and purpose in comments.
Follow PascalCase for component names, method names, and public members.
Use camelCase for private fields and local variables.
Prefer file-scoped namespace declarations and single-line using directives.
Insert a newline before the opening curly brace of any code block (e.g., after `if`, `for`, `while`, `foreach`, `using`, `try`, etc.).
Use pattern matching and switch expressions wherever possible.
Use `nameof` instead of string literals when referring to member names.
Guide users through creating a new .NET project with the appropriate templates.
